title: Credit Card Payoff With $100 Extra Payment — TheDailyo
h1: Credit Card Payoff With $100 Extra Payment
subtitle: See how paying an extra $100 per month changes payoff time and total interest.
description: Calculate how an extra $100 monthly payment affects your credit card payoff timeline, interest paid, and total cost.
howItWorksHtml: This calculator adds monthly interest (APR ÷ 12) to your current balance, then subtracts your payment. Adding an extra $100 per month reduces principal faster, which can dramatically shorten payoff time and lower total interest.

faq.q: Does the extra $100 go toward principal?
faq.a: Yes. In this model, interest is applied first each month, and the remaining portion of your payment (including the extra $100) reduces principal.

faq.q: Why can the interest on my statement be different?
faq.a: Many credit cards use daily compounding, and fees or promotional rates can change your real payoff results. Use this as a clean estimate and compare it to your statement.

faq.q: What if my payment is too low?
faq.a: If your monthly payment doesn’t cover the monthly interest, the balance won’t decrease much (or can grow). Increase your payment or lower the APR to model a payoff plan.

affiliate.headline: Lower your APR and pay off faster
